tldr: Continue is an OSS AI coding assistant for IDEs; Composio is an AI-agent tool-integration platform. They sit in different layers, so 'migration' usually means shifting from in-IDE coding help to building agents that act on third-party apps.
why_switch: Your need shifted from IDE coding help to agent automation
You want managed connectors for many third-party apps
You are building autonomous workflows, not editing code
You want usage-based pricing instead of seat pricing
You need agent SDKs across multiple languages
what_to_export: Chat history if you want to retain it
export_method: Continue's configuration lives in plain files under your home directory (typically ~/.continue/config.json or config.ts) and per-workspace overrides. Save a copy of these files for reference. Note the model providers and keys you used, since they are not portable to Composio. If you rely on team policies in Continue's paid plan, capture those settings from its dashboard. Treat chat history as local files you may archive. There is no formal export command for Continue beyond copying config and any custom prompt files you wrote.
import_method: Composio is consumed via SDKs and a dashboard rather than config files. Sign up at composio.dev, create a project, and connect the third-party apps your agent needs through Composio's connector catalogue. Install the Composio SDK in your agent code and authenticate. Define the tools your agent should expose, then call them from your LLM loop. Continue's IDE coding help has no Composio equivalent; if you still need it, keep Continue for that flow and use Composio strictly for the agent action layer.
gotchas: These are different layers; confirm Composio actually fits the use case.
You will still need an LLM provider, billed separately.
Composio adds usage-based fees on top of model costs.
Each connector requires its own auth setup.
No automated import from Continue config to Composio projects.
If teams still need IDE help, you may need to run both tools.

email.subject: Plan: moving from Continue to Composio for agent workflows
email.body:
Hi team,

Over the next 14 days we will pilot Composio for agent workflows that act on third-party apps, while reviewing what stays on Continue for in-IDE coding help. The two products serve different layers, so this is a scope realignment.

If you depend on Continue features beyond IDE chat, please flag them so we can plan accordingly.

Thanks,
Your customer
